title: Operations — Patch Compliance Lapse via Silent Un-Patching
description: An engineer (or AI agent) configures `Ec2PatchBaseline` with `rebootOption: { kind: "NoReboot" }` at startup-hardened tier, intending to avoid disrupting tenants. The component refuses construction (M7 invariant), but the threat path remains real: a sandbox-tier consumer might accept silent un-patching, leaving a kernel CVE exposed for sixty days. Target end state matches Hulumi's `Ec2PatchBaseline` (M7) + `Ec2PatchWaves` (M7) + `HulumiOperationsHardeningPack`'s `O_PATCH_1` (M10). Threats focus on the gap between an EC2 patch announcement and the cluster reboot that activates it.
actors: AI Coding Agent (trusted, but may suggest NoReboot for tenant-stability reasons)
External Attacker (post-patch-announcement, racing to exploit before reboot)
assets: EC2 instance kernel and userspace package state

stride.name: Tampered Patch:Group tag bypasses the wave-composer
stride.description: An attacker (or a misconfigured IaC author) sets a Patch:Group value outside {dev, staging, production} on an EC2 instance. The MaintenanceWindowTarget no longer matches; the instance is silently excluded from patching. Mitigation: `O_PATCH_1` (M10) rejects the tag value at preview; SCP-style guardrails on `ec2:CreateTags` for that tag complement at runtime.

stride.name: Repudiation via missing compliance metric
stride.description: Without `complianceMetric.topicArn` wired to a real SNS, patch-compliance regressions surface as a CloudWatch metric nobody reads. Mitigation: `Ec2PatchBaseline` requires the SNS ARN (M7 invariant); the alarm fires when non-compliant instance count >= 1.

stride.name: Information disclosure via NoReboot pile-up
stride.description: A `NoReboot` posture at startup-hardened tier means kernel CVEs accumulate without activation. `Ec2PatchBaseline` refuses this combination at construction (M7 type system); the sandbox-only escape requires a written `hulumi_decision_comment` (>= 8 chars) tagged on the resource for audit.

stride.name: Denial via synchronized-reboot storm
stride.description: Without CRC32-bucket staggering, all instances reboot at the maintenance window's start, taking the service offline simultaneously. Mitigation: `staggering.bucketCount` defaults to 4 and is bound at 16; max-concurrency on the run-command task scales inversely with bucket count.

stride.name: Elevation via unpatched root-exploit kernel
stride.description: An unpatched local-privilege-escalation kernel exploit lets a compromised non-root process become root. Sixty days is the empirical compromise window. Mitigation: tier-aware patch approval delay (3 days at startup-hardened, 7 at sandbox).

abuseCases.title: NoReboot at startup-hardened tier
abuseCases.description: Mitigation: M7 constructor refuses the combination.
